contentBlocks.original:
नमः कृष्णाय नीलाय शिखिखण्डनिभाय च ।
नमो नीलमधूकाय नीलोत्पलनिभाय च ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namaḥ kṛṣṇāya nīlāya śikhikhaṇḍanibhāya cha ।
namō nīlamadhūkāya nīlōtpalanibhāya cha ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the dark, the blue-black, resembling the peacock's feather; salutations to the dark honey-tree-blue, resembling the blue lotus.

contentBlocks.original:
नमो निर्मांसदेहाय दीर्घश्रुतिजटाय च ।
नमो विशालनेत्राय शुष्कोदर भयानक ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namō nirmāṃsadēhāya dīrghaśrutijaṭāya cha ।
namō viśālanētrāya śuṣkōdara bhayānaka ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the fleshless-bodied, with long-tufted matted locks; salutations to the wide-eyed, O terrifying one with a shrunken belly.

contentBlocks.original:
नमः पौरुषगात्राय स्थूलरोमाय ते नमः ।
नमो नित्यं क्षुधार्ताय नित्यतृप्ताय ते नमः ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namaḥ pauruṣagātrāya sthūlarōmāya tē namaḥ ।
namō nityaṃ kṣudhārtāya nityatṛptāya tē namaḥ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one of virile form, with thick body-hair — salutations to you; salutations to the eternally hungry, and salutations to the eternally satisfied.

contentBlocks.original:
नमो घोराय रौद्राय भीषणाय करालिने ।
नमो दीर्घाय शुष्काय कालदंष्ट्र नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namō ghōrāya raudrāya bhīṣaṇāya karāḻinē ।
namō dīrghāya śuṣkāya kāladaṃṣṭra namō'stu tē ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the terrible, the fierce, the fearsome, the formidable; salutations to the tall, the lean, O fierce-toothed one — salutations to you.

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्ते घोररूपाय दुर्निरीक्ष्याय ते नमः ।
नमस्ते सर्वभक्षाय वलीमुख नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastē ghōrarūpāya durnirīkṣyāya tē namaḥ ।
namastē sarvabhakṣāya valīmukha namō'stu tē ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to you of terrible form, O one hard to behold; salutations to you the all-devouring, O wrinkle-faced one — salutations.

contentBlocks.original:
सूर्यपुत्त्र नमस्तेऽस्तु भास्वरोभयदायिने ।
अधोदृष्टे नमस्तेऽस्तु संवर्तक नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sūryaputtra namastē'stu bhāsvarōbhayadāyinē ।
adhōdṛṣṭē namastē'stu saṃvartaka namō'stu tē ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O son of the Sun, salutations — O giver of fear to the luminous; O downward-gazing one, salutations — O Samvartaka (dissolver), salutations to you.

contentBlocks.original:
नमो मन्दगते तुभ्यं निष्प्रभाय नमोनमः ।
तपसा ज्ञानदेहाय नित्ययोगरताय च ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namō mandagatē tubhyaṃ niṣprabhāya namōnamaḥ ।
tapasā jñānadēhāya nityayōgaratāya cha ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to you the slow-moving, O you of little lustre; to the one whose body is pure knowledge, ever devoted to yogic discipline.

contentBlocks.original:
ज्ञानचक्षुर्नमस्तेऽस्तु काश्यपात्मजसूनवे ।
तुष्टो ददासि राज्यं त्वं क्रुद्धो हरसि तत्‍ क्षणात् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jñānachakṣurnamastē'stu kāśyapātmajasūnavē ।
tuṣṭō dadāsi rājyaṃ tvaṃ kruddhō harasi tat‍ kṣaṇāt ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O eye of wisdom, salutations — O son of the son of Kashyapa; when pleased you bestow kingdoms; when angered you snatch them away in an instant.
Gods, asuras and humans, siddhas, vidyadharas and nagas — all who are gazed upon by you, O Saura (Saturn), swiftly fall into distress.

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मा शक्रोयमश्चैव मुनयः सप्ततारकाः ।
राज्यभ्रष्टाः पतन्तीह तव दृष्ट्याऽवलोकितः ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmā śakrōyamaśchaiva munayaḥ saptatārakāḥ ।
rājyabhraṣṭāḥ patantīha tava dṛṣṭyā'valōkitaḥ ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Brahma, Indra, Yama and even the seven great sages — having been gazed upon by you they fall from their kingdoms.

contentBlocks.original:
त्वयाऽवलोकितास्तेऽपि नाशं यान्ति समूलतः ।
प्रसादं कुरु मे सौरे प्रणत्वाहित्वमर्थितः ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvayā'valōkitāstē'pi nāśaṃ yānti samūlataḥ ।
prasādaṃ kuru mē saurē praṇatvāhitvamarthitaḥ ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Even those gazed upon by you perish root and branch. Be gracious to me, O Saura — I bow to you with a humble prayer.
